L1.area: N-dimensional image processing and measurement operations

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in a Python C extension where a reference count on a result object is prematurely decremented during an error-handling code path. If an error occurs during processing, the object may be freed while still referenced, allowing subsequent access to freed memory and potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: N-dimensional image processing C extension modules for labeled-array and measurement operations
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the N-dimensional image processing C extension where a reference count on a result list object is prematurely decremented in an error-handling code path. When an error occurs while processing labeled regions in an N-dimensional array, the result object may be freed while still referenced, allowing access to freed memory and potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: A premature Py_XDECREF call on a result list object in the error-handling path of a C extension function causes a use-after-free, allowing potential access to freed memory.
